description: Theme-driven slide design system: themes fully own slide backgrounds, typography, and density. Slides are classified by section type (title/content/closing) and styled automatically. Agents pick a theme, not hex colors.

userStories.title: Add slide tokens to Theme dataclass
userStories.description: As a developer, I need slide-specific design tokens on the Theme dataclass so each theme can control slide backgrounds, text colors, padding, and chart sizing.
acceptanceCriteria: Theme dataclass gains 8 new fields with defaults: slide_title_bg (str), slide_title_text (str), slide_content_bg (str), slide_content_text (str), slide_closing_bg (str), slide_closing_text (str), slide_padding (str, default '48px'), slide_max_chart_height (str, default '55cqh')
All new fields have defaults so existing Theme instantiations still work
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Set slide token values for Corporate, Executive, and Financial themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the first three themes to have distinct slide palettes.
acceptanceCriteria: Corporate theme: slide_title_bg='#1e293b', slide_title_text='#f8fafc', slide_content_bg='#ffffff', slide_content_text='#0f172a', slide_closing_bg='#f8fafc', slide_closing_text='#0f172a'
Executive theme: slide_title_bg='#0f172a', slide_title_text='#f8fafc', slide_content_bg='#ffffff', slide_content_text='#0f172a', slide_closing_bg='#f1f5f9', slide_closing_text='#0f172a'
Financial theme: slide_title_bg='#1e3a5f', slide_title_text='#e2e8f0', slide_content_bg='#ffffff', slide_content_text='#0f172a', slide_closing_bg='#f8fafc', slide_closing_text='#0f172a'
DEFAULT_THEME (alias for Corporate) also has the slide tokens
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Set slide token values for Consulting, Technical, Editorial, and Dark themes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the remaining four themes to have distinct slide palettes.
acceptanceCriteria: Consulting theme: slide_title_bg='#0f172a', slide_title_text='#f8fafc', slide_content_bg='#ffffff', slide_content_text='#0f172a', slide_closing_bg='#f0fdf4', slide_closing_text='#0f172a'
Technical theme: slide_title_bg='#18181b', slide_title_text='#fafafa', slide_content_bg='#ffffff', slide_content_text='#18181b', slide_closing_bg='#fafafa', slide_closing_text='#18181b'
Editorial theme: slide_title_bg='#1c1917', slide_title_text='#fef3c7', slide_content_bg='#fffbf5', slide_content_text='#1c1917', slide_closing_bg='#fef3c7', slide_closing_text='#1c1917'
Dark theme: slide_title_bg='#020617', slide_title_text='#e2e8f0', slide_content_bg='#0f172a', slide_content_text='#e2e8f0', slide_closing_bg='#1e293b', slide_closing_text='#e2e8f0'
All 7 themes in THEMES dict have slide token values set
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add slide role classification function to renderers
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a function that classifies a slide's role (title/content/closing) based on the section types it contains.
acceptanceCriteria: New function _classify_slide_role(section: dict) -> str in renderers.py
Returns 'title' if any child section has type 'summary-header'
Returns 'closing' if any child section has type 'action-items' or 'key-takeaway'
Returns 'content' for all other slides
If slide has both summary-header and action-items, 'title' wins (first match)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update _render_slide to use theme slide tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the slide renderer to assign backgrounds and text colors from the theme instead of reading background_color from the section data.
acceptanceCriteria: _render_slide calls _classify_slide_role to determine slide role
Slide background set from theme: slide_title_bg, slide_content_bg, or slide_closing_bg based on role
data-background-color attribute set to the theme-determined background (for frontend dark detection)
Slide section element gets inline style with background-color and padding from theme
Child sections rendered with the appropriate text color context for the slide role
The background_color field in the section dict is ignored (backwards-compatible)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update slideshow auto-wrap to use theme slide tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the auto-wrap logic in render_structured_to_html to use theme slide_content_bg instead of hardcoded #ffffff.
acceptanceCriteria: In render_structured_to_html, when auto-wrapping non-slide sections for slideshow, use theme slide_content_bg instead of '#ffffff'
Auto-wrapped slides still get correct content role classification
Existing slideshow rendering produces valid HTML
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Update SKILL.md slideshow documentation
userStories.description: As an agent, I need updated skill.md docs that explain theme-controlled slide backgrounds and remove background_color from slide section docs.
acceptanceCriteria: Slide section type table no longer lists background_color as a field
New subsection explains theme-slide interaction: title slides (summary-header) get dark bg, content slides get light bg, closing slides (action-items/key-takeaway) get accent bg
Slide examples updated to not include background_color
Themes table updated to note slide palette personalities
HTML mode slideshow note updated to remove data-background-color agent guidance
SKILL.md version bumped
Existing content preserved

userStories.title: Remove background_color from seed slideshow data
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the seed slideshows to rely on theme-assigned backgrounds instead of hardcoded background_color values.
acceptanceCriteria: All background_color fields removed from slide sections in seed.py slideshow configs
Both seed slideshows (Q1 Competitive Landscape, Board Strategy Update) updated
Seed runs without errors: uv run python -m app.seed
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
